description_long: This is a classic upright arcade game cabinet with a wooden exterior that shows significant wear, scratches, and patches of discoloration, suggesting heavy use or age. The front features a large, slightly recessed display screen with a cracked or damaged appearance, surrounded by a dark bezel. Below the screen is a slanted control panel equipped with two joysticks and multiple colored buttons, arranged for two-player gameplay, with red and yellow buttons being prominent. The lower front section houses a visible coin slot mechanism, typical of coin-operated arcade machines. The side panels are plain, with a gradient of dark wear and no visible artwork or decals, and the back is similarly unadorned but heavily marked with scuffs and stains. The overall structure is boxy and robust, designed to stand on the floor and withstand frequent public use.
description: A worn, wooden upright arcade game cabinet with a cracked screen, dual joysticks, colored buttons, and a visible coin slot. The sides are plain and heavily scuffed, with no artwork.
description_view_0: Front view showing the cracked display screen, dual joysticks, colored buttons, and the coin slot at the bottom.
description_view_1: Left side view highlighting the plain, darkly worn side panel and the angled control panel.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the back panel with extensive scuff marks and no visible access panels or artwork.
description_view_3: Right side view similar to the left, showing the plain, darkly worn side panel and the slanted control panel.
attribute_screen_type: CRT, curved, medium size, with a thick bezel and visible screen damage
attribute_control_panel_layout: Two joysticks and six colored buttons (three red, three yellow) arranged for two-player side-by-side play, with controls symmetrically placed
